rulings.Case Title: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., ACQUIRER OF CERTAIN VS. MOSSER VICTORIAN HOTEL OF ARTS AND MUSIC, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For An Order 1) Modifying The Receivership Order; And 2) Authorizing Court-Appointed Receiver To Sell Property Located At 54 4th Street, San Francisco, California 94103
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 18, 2026. Line 2. PLAINTIFF JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A., Motion For An Order 1) Modifying The Receivership Order; And 2) Authorizing Court-Appointed Receiver To Sell Property is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to provide any evidentiary support of an urgent fiscal or physical need warranting a sale. (Cal-American Income Property Fund VII v. Brown Development Corp. rulings.Case Title: WALTER MOREANO VS. ALBERTO TRUJEQUE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena And Request For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 18, 2026. Line 6. DEFENDANT ALBERTO TRUJEQUE, HOLANDA PEREZ Motion To Quash Subpoena And Request For Attorney'S Fees And Costs is DENIED. The trial date was continued to June 1, 2026 for the specific purpose of allowing Plaintiff to conduct discovery on Defendants' affirmative defenses that were revived as a result of the granting of Defendants' request to withdraw their admissions on the day of trial. The subsequent denial of Defendants' motion to reopen all discovery in this action does not invalidate the conditions imposed on the granting of Defendants' motion to withdraw their admissions. It appears from Defendants' moving papers that Defendants intentionally omitted any reference to the reason for the continuance to June 1, 2026 in order to mislead the Court regarding the facts and procedural history relevant to this motion, and gain an unfair advantage from the relief Judge Haines previously granted to them. Plaintiff's request for sanctions in opposition is denied without prejudice, as Plaintiff cited no legal authority for this request. =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRICK & TIMBER, INC. VS. SONIA TRUDEAU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Plaintiff To: 1) Compel Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One) Without Objection; Compel Responses By Defendant To Form Interrogatories-General (Set One); Compel Responses By Defendant To Special Interrogatories (Set One) Without Objection; Compel Responses By Defendant To Request For Production Of Documents (Set One) Without Objection; 2) Deem Admitted By Defendant Requests For Admission (Set One), And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 18, 2026. Line 8. Plaintiff's Motion to: 1) Compel Responses to Interrogatories and Request for Production; 2) Deem Admissions, and for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED in part. Defendant shall serve complete verified responses without objection to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories-Unlawful Detainer (Set One); Form Interrogatories-General (Set One); Special Interrogatories (Set One); and Request for Production of Documents (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Request for deemed admissions is DENIED, as Defendant has served a substantially compliant response before the hearing date. Sanctions granted in the amount of $810. Provided, however, that the sanctions will be waived if Defendant timely serves responses to all outstanding discovery in compliance with this order. =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. LEANDRA DILLON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 18, 2026. Line 10.1. DEFENDANT LEANDRA DILLON DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer from notice of entry of order. California Business & Professions Code 10131(b) does not stand for the specific proposition for which Defendant cites it, and Defendant provides no other authority to support this proposition. As this proposition is the basis for Defendant's demurrer, Defendant has failed to show that the Complaint is defective. =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. LEANDRA DILLON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 18, 2026. Line 10.2. DEFENDANT LEANDRA DILLON MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED, 5 days to answer from notice of entry of order. Defendant does not dispute that the Complaint is verified by the Plaintiff. Plaintiff is the party that filed the Complaint. While Defendant's argument that Plaintiff lacks standing is the basis for the concurrently-filed demurrer, this does not mean Plaintiff's verification of its own Complaint is not drawn in conformity with CCP 446. =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JOAN RYAN VS. PATRICIA LYON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 18, 2026. Line 12. DEFENDANT PATRICIA LYON, TOM LILEY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Plaintiff pled sufficient facts in the Complaint to state a cause of action for unlawful detainer pursuant to Civil Code 3479 and Rent Ordinance 37.9(a)(3), and Plaintiff was not required to give an option to cure pursuant to Civil Code 1946.1(h) or C.C.P. 1161(4). =(501/DWH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
